coredata.dc:title: Synthesis and spectral analysis of fluorescent probes for Ce4+ and OCl− ions based on fluorescein Schiff base with amino or hydrazine structure: Application in actual water samples and biological imaging
coredata.prism:publicationName: Spectrochimica Acta Part A: Molecular and Biomolecular Spectroscopy
coredata.dc:creator: Yan, Fanyong
Fan, Keqing
Ma, Tengchuang
Xu, Jinxia
Wang, Jie
Ma, Cong
coredata.dc:description: Two Schiff base fluorescein probes (FDA, FDH) based on fluorescein-aldehyde and nitroaniline derivatives were synthesized. The effects of amino and hydrazine substituents in fluorescein backbones were examined via fluorescence and absorbance spectra. In the presence of Ce4+, the fluorescence of FDA was quenched due to the ligand to metal charge transfer (LMCT). Hypochloric acid can react with the CN bond, and blocking the photo induced electron transfer (PET) of FDH leads to enhancement of the fluorescence. FDA showed detection limits for Ce4+ and OCl− as low as 63 nM in concentration range of 0–4 μM. FDH showed detection limits for OCl− as low as 0.8 μM in concentration rang 0–100 μM. Polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF) membrane containing the probes was prepared for the real-time qualitative detection of Ce4+ and OCl− in real water samples. The probes were successfully applied to biological imaging in vascular smooth muscle cells (VSMCs) and are expected to find applications in biosensing.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Fluorescein
Nitroaniline derivatives
Schiff base
Cerium ion
Hypochloric acid
Application

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Fluorescence Detection of Ce4+ and OCl− in Environment Water Samples and PVDF Membrane
Synthesis and Fluorescence Changes of FDA and FDH
Spectroscopic Properties Detection of Various Ions by FDA and FDH
The Investigation of pH and Fluorescence Decay Curves Measurements
Real Sample Analysis and Sensing Studies With PVDF Membrane

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: The absorbance spectra and digital photographs under normal light (a) FDA (1 μM) upon the addition of Ce4+ (0–4 μM). (b) FDH (10 μM) upon the addition of OCl− (0–100 μM). (For interpretation of the references to color in this figure, the reader is referred to the web version of this article.)
Fluorescence response of probes and digital photographs under UV–vis light (a) FDA (1 μM) in the presence of 4 μM of Ce4+. (b) FDH (10 μM) in the presence of 100 μM of OCl−.

(a) Effect of various metal ions on the fluorescence intensity of FDA (1 μM). (b) Fluorescence spectra of FDA in the presence of Ce4+ with the addition of competitive metal ions. (c) Effect of various anions on the fluorescence intensity of FDH (10 μM). (d) Fluorescence spectra of FDA in the presence of OCl− with the addition of competitive anions.

(a) The fluorescence spectra of FDA (1 μM) upon the addition of Ce4+. Inset: plot of fluorescence intensity ratios of F0 − F at 505 nm versus the concentration of Ce4+. (b) CIE chromaticity coordinates of FDA for the detection of Ce4+. (c) The fluorescence spectra of FDH (10 μM) upon the addition of OCl−. Inset: plot of fluorescence intensity ratios of F − F0 at 515 nm versus the concentration of OCl−. (d) CIE chromaticity coordinates of FDH for the detection of OCl−. (For interpretation of the references to color in this figure, the reader is referred to the web version of this article.)

(a) Fluorescence intensity of free FDA (1 μM) and FDA + Ce4+ (4 μM) in water with different pH. (b) Fluorescence intensity of free FDH (10 μM) and FDH + OCl− (100 μM) in water with different pH. (c) Fluorescence decay curves of FDA (1 μM) and FDA + Ce4+. (d) Fluorescence decay curves of FDH (10 μM) and FDH + OCl−.
(a) Job's plot of the complex formed by [Ce4+]/[Ce4+] + [FDA]. The total concentration of FDA and Ce4+ was 2 μM. (b) Benesie-Hildebrand expression for FDA with different concentration Ce4+.
Frontier molecular orbital profiles of (a) FDA, FDA-Ce4+ and (b) FDH, FDH-OCl− based on DFT.

Inverted fluorescence microscope images bright-field (top), and fluorescence (bottom) of VMSCs: (a) incubated with FDA (1 μM); (b) incubated with FDA (1 μM) and then incubated with 4 μM Ce4+; (c) incubated with FDH (10 μM); (d) incubated with FDH (10 μM) and then incubated with 100 μM OCl−.
Syntheses of probes FDA and FDH.
Proposed mechanism between FDA and Ce4+.
Proposed mechanism between FDH and OCl−.
Detection of Ce4+ spiked in tap water and real lake water samples.
Detection of OCl− spiked in tap water and real lake water samples.

head.ce:title: Synthesis and spectral analysis of fluorescent probes for Ce4+ and OCl− ions based on fluorescein Schiff base with amino or hydrazine structure: Application in actual water samples and biological imaging

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: State Key Laboratory of Separation Membranes and Membrane Processes, Tianjin Engineering Center for Safety Evaluation of Water Quality & Safeguards Technology, College of Environmental and Chemical Engineering, Tianjin Polytechnic University, Tianjin 300387, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: State Key Laboratory of Separation Membranes and Membrane Processes
Tianjin Engineering Center for Safety Evaluation of Water Quality & Safeguards Technology
College of Environmental and Chemical Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Nuclear Medicine, Harbin Medical University Cancer Hospital, Harbin, Heilongjiang 150081, China

ce:footnote.ce:note-para: Author Contributions. Fanyong Yan and Keqing Fan contributed equally.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Two Schiff base fluorescein probes (FDA, FDH) based on fluorescein-aldehyde and nitroaniline derivatives were synthesized. The effects of amino and hydrazine substituents in fluorescein backbones were examined via fluorescence and absorbance spectra. In the presence of Ce4+, the fluorescence of FDA was quenched due to the ligand to metal charge transfer (LMCT). Hypochloric acid can react with the CN bond, and blocking the photo induced electron transfer (PET) of FDH leads to enhancement of the fluorescence. FDA showed detection limits for Ce4+ and OCl− as low as 63 nM in concentration range of 0–4 μM. FDH showed detection limits for OCl− as low as 0.8 μM in concentration rang 0–100 μM. Polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF) membrane containing the probes was prepared for the real-time qualitative detection of Ce4+ and OCl− in real water samples. The probes were successfully applied to biological imaging in vascular smooth muscle cells (VSMCs) and are expected to find applications in biosensing.

ce:section.ce:para: Ce4+ is a kind of rare earth element, which is widely used in electronics, chemical industry and medicine due to its special physical and chemical properties and good biological affinity. In addition, Ce4+ is one of the most important oxidants in lanthanide ions and can react with a variety of biological molecules including DNA and proteins [1]. Excess Ce4+ can cause serious damage to biological systems [2,3]. Hypochlorous is present in human body as an important reactive oxygen species [4]. The strong oxidation of OCl− makes it can resistant to bacterial invasion and regulate cell life cycle [5]. Excessive OCl− can cause skin tissue, lung injury and some diseases such as cardiovascular, arthritis and atherosclerosis [6]. The World Health Organization proposed that the minimum residual concentration of OCl− in drinking water should be 3.3 μM [7]. As Ce4+ and OCl− are crucial in many biological environmental and biological processes, fluorescent probes for Ce4+ and OCl− need to be developed in order to study their impact on the environment and human health. Some fluorescent probes for detecting Ce4+ or OCl− have been synthesized, but the probes need complicated synthesis, high organic solvent ratio, and long reaction time [1,6–8]. So, it is necessary to develop fluorescent probes that can respond quickly and have good water solubility.

Among the existing detection technologies, fluorescence analysis has received extensive attention because of its advantages of high sensitivity, simple operation, fast analysis, and no damage [9–12]. The Schiff base fluorescein probes retain the water solubility, strong fluorescence, visible excitation and emission of fluorescein, and can provide more reactions and binding sites due to the high activity of the CN bond. Meng et al. [13] and Diao et al. [14] synthesized Schiff base probes for Fe3+ and Al3+ both containing the amino substituents. Kambam et al. [15] and Chen et al. [8] synthesized Schiff base probes for Au3+ and OCl− both containing the hydrazine substituents. To compare and investigate the effect of amino and hydrazine substituents on the fluorescence properties of probes, the probes containing the imine structure were constructed by 2,4-dinitroaniline, 2,4-dinitrophenylhydrazine and fluorescein aldehyde. The two probes have different optical phenomena and electron cloud distribution, which caused by the different amino and hydrazine substituents in fluorescein backbones.

Herein, two probes (FDA, FDH) containing Schiff base structure were synthesized through the reaction between the fluorescein aldehyde and nitroaniline derivatives. (Scheme 1 ).The effect of amino and hydrazine groups on the fluorescence properties of the probes were investigated by fluorescence spectrometry. Interestingly, the difference in the structure of FDA and FDH, which leads to a completely opposite fluorescence phenomenon. The green fluorescent maximum at 505 nm (λex = 445 nm) of FDA was quenched by Ce4+. FDH contains NN structure, which conforms to the electronic cloud distribution of the PET process. Therefore, the initial FDH only has very weak fluorescence. After OCl− was added to the FDH, the fluorescent intensity increased sharply. FDA and FDH has high water solubility and biocompatibility, so they were successfully applied to biological imaging in VSMCs. They are expected to be further applied in the field of biosensing and drug release monitoring in future.

Fluorescein (90%), 2,4-dinitroaniline (95%) and 2,4-dinitrophenylhydrazine (95%) were purchased from Aladdin Reagent Co. (Shanghai, China). Metal salts and other anion salts were purchased from Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd. (Shanghai, China). All chemicals were analytical grade and used without further depuration [16]. Double-distilled water was used throughout all experiment. PVDF membrane was purchased from Tianjin Motian Membrane Technology Co. (Tianjin, China).

Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy (FT-IR) measurements were conducted by TENSOR37 Fourier transform infrared spectrometer from Tianjin Guangdong Scientific and Technological Development Co., Ltd. Absorption spectra were gained on UV–vis spectrometer (TU-1901, Japan). Fluorescence spectra were carried out on F-380 fluorescence spectrophotometer from Tianjin Guangdong Scientific and Technological Development Co., Ltd. 1H NMR spectra were measured with Bruker Advance IIWB 400 MHz spectrometer (in CD3SOCD3, TMS as an internal standard). Using Bruker micro TOF-QII instrument to test mass spectra data. The pH measurements were carried out on a Mettler Toledo pH meter [16]. Fluorescence lifetimes were recorded on FLS980 (Edinburgh, UK). Cellular imaging was performed using Olympus Zeiss 710 inverted fluorescence microscope (Germany).

The synthesis routine is shown in Scheme 1. Fluorescein aldehyde was synthesized based on the previous method [17]. 0.20 g of fluorescein aldehyde (0.56 mmol), 0.10 g of 2,4-dinitroaniline (0.56 mmol), and 0.11 g of 2,4-dinitrophenylhydrazine (0.56 mmol) were dissolved in 30 mL of ethanol, respectively. Then 10 drops of glacial acetic acid were added to the solution, the mixtures were stirred and refluxed for about 48 h [18]. The reaction solvent was removed by vacuum distillation, and the crude products were purified by column chromatography (CHCl3:CHOH, 10: 1, v/v) [19].

The probes FDA and FDH were dissolved in 5% ethanol and obtained the stock solutions with a concentration of 0.25 mM, respectively [20].. Stock solutions of metal ions (Ce4+, Ce3+, Al3+, La3+, Fe3+, Hg2+, Cu2+,Co2+, Ni2+, Mn2+, Pb2+, Zn2+, and Ca2+, 2 × 10−3 M). Stock solutions of anions (OCl−, H2PO4 −, HPO4 2−, F−, Cl−, Br−, I−, S2−, S2O8 2−, S2O3 2−, SO3 2−, SO4 2−, CH3COO−, NO3 −, NO2 − and H2O2, 2 × 10−3 M). One of the most basic requirements for the probe detection is that it can't be interfered by common metal ions, which are potentially competitive in the environment. As a result of these ions are common anions and cations in life, they play a control group in the selectivity test of the probe. Pipetting 40 μL FDA stock solution or 400 μL FDH stock solution into a 10 mL volumetric flask to acquire probes test solution, which were added with different analytes with 10 mM PBS buffer (pH 7.4) to volume [21].

The purpose is to dilute the high concentration stock solution to 1 μM (FDA) and 10 μM (FDH). UV–vis and fluorescence emission spectra were recorded at room temperature. In fluorescence spectroscopy (λex = 445 nm), the fluorescence emission range was chosen to be 450–650 nm [22]. The excitation and emission slits were set at 5 nm.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fluorescence Detection of Ce4+ and OCl− in Environment Water Samples and PVDF Membrane
ce:section.ce:para: The detection application of Ce4+ and OCl− was studied in tap water, Tuanbo Lake and swimming pool. All water samples were from Tianjin, China. 40 μL of FDA solution were mixed with 10 mL of filtered water samples and different concentrations of Ce4+ solution. 400 μL FDH solution were mixed with 10 mL of filtered water samples and different concentrations of OCl− solution.

PVDF membrane was also prepared for Ce4+ and OCl− on-site testing, which is used to assess the practical value of the probes. In order to attach the probes evenly to the test strip, the PVDF membrane were soaked in 5% methanol solution in FDA (1 mM) or FDH (10 mM) for 10 min and were dried, respectively [23]. Then, the solution which contained the corresponding Ce4+ (5 mM) or OCl− (20 mM) are equably sprayed on the PVDF membrane.

VSMCs were cultured in the in Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium which contains 10% bovine serum and were incubated in incubators at 37 °C and 5% CO2 for 24 h [24]. The cells were dispersed into a single cell suspension by parenzyme and then cultured with a six-well plate. After 24 h, the medium was discarded, and the probes solution were added to continue the incubation for 30 min and then imaged under an inverted fluorescence microscope, respectively.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synthesis and Fluorescence Changes of FDA and FDH
ce:section.ce:para: FDA was obtained based on Schiff base reaction between 2,4-dinitroaniline and fluorescein monoaldehyde in ethanol. FDH was obtained based on Schiff base reaction between 2,4-dinitrophenylhydrazine and fluorescein monoaldehyde in ethanol (Scheme 1). The structures of FDA and FDH were characterized by 1H NMR. The optical properties of two probes were studied by fluorescence spectroscopy. It is found that the two probes have different optical phenomena and electron cloud distribution, which was caused by the different amino or hydrazine structure. The green fluorescent maximum at 505 nm (λex = 445 nm) of FDA was quenched by Ce4+. FDH contains NN structure, which conforms to the electronic cloud distribution of the PET process. Therefore, the initial FDH only has very weak fluorescence. After OCl− was added to the FDH, the fluorescent intensity increased sharply. The purpose of this work is to compare the effects of different structures on the performance of the probes.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Spectroscopic Properties Detection of Various Ions by FDA and FDH
ce:section.ce:para: The optical properties of the probes can be investigated by measuring and recording the UV–vis and fluorescence emission spectra. Upon addition different concentration of Ce4+ ion to FDA solution, the fluorescein-based characteristic absorption peak at 490 nm was significantly increased, revealing the formation of a FDA-Ce4+ coordination. Meanwhile, broad absorption bands increased in the 400–450 nm range was also observed in UV–vis titrations due to the increasing solvated Ce4+ species.

Initial FDH displayed a xanthene moiety based characteristic absorption peak at 331 nm. The ring-opening fluorescein based characteristic absorption peak (493 nm) was not found, indicating that the initial FDH was in the form of lactam. With the increasing of OCl−, the peak at 331 nm decreased, and a new absorption peak at 493 nm appeared, and both absorption bands show a red shift. The red shift of the absorption band was accompanied by the change of the solution color from brown to green (Fig. 1b, inset), which can be observed by naked eye under normal light [25].

Fluorescence detection of FDA and FDH for Ce4+ and OCl− was studied in 10 mM PBS buffer (pH = 7.4). When excited at 445 nm, the FDA solution showed maximum green fluorescent (Φ = 0.87) at 505 nm. After addition of 4 μM Ce4+ around 10 min, fluorescence of FDA was quenched about 10-fold (Fig. 2a). In the presence of 100 μM OCl−, the fluorescent intensity of FDH increased rapidly at 515 nm (from Φ = 0.13 to Φ = 0.79) with excitation wavelength at 445 nm. The fluorescent responses quickly achieved with addition of Ce4+ or OCl− about 5 min.

In the fluorescence test of selectivity and anti-jamming ability, FDA (1 μM) and FDH (10 μM) showed good selectivity and anti-interference ability to Ce4+ and OCl− respectively. The fluorescence selectivity of FDA (1 μM) to various interfering metal ions (Ce4+, Ce3+, Al3+, La3+, Fe3+, Hg2+, Cu2+, Co2+, Ni2+, Mn2+, Pb2+, Zn2+, and Ca2+, 2 × 10−3 M) was recorded. As shown in Fig. 3(a, b), only the introduction of Ce4+ led to remarkable decrease of fluorescence at 505 nm, which obviously implied the high selectivity was then conducted by putting Ce4+ into FDA solution containing other metal ions. The results demonstrated that FDA can effectively detect Ce4+ in the presence of other interfering metal ions. As shown in Fig. 3(c, d), the fluorescence intensity of FDH was not increased with the addition same amount of other anion ions (H2PO4 −, HPO4 2−, F−, Cl−, Br−, I−, S2−, S2O8 2−, S2O3 2−, SO3 2−, SO4 2−, CH3COO−, NO3 −, NO2 − and H2O2, 2 × 10−3 M). Addition of OCl− into FDH solution in the presence of other interfering anions made no obvious fluorescence change of FDH. The above results indicated that FDA and FDH showed highly sensitivity and selectivity for detection.

To quantitatively evaluate the change fluorescence, the fluorescence intensity of FDA (1 μM) with different mounts of Ce4+ and FDH (10 μM) with different mounts of OCl− was investigated. Fig. 4(a) presented the fluorescence intensity of FDA in 505 nm gradually decreased with the increasing of the concentration of Ce4+ (0–4 μM) and the fluorescence intensity of FDH in 515 nm gradually enhanced with concentration of OCl− from 0 to 100 μM (Fig. 4c). The results displayed good linear relationship of the common [F0-F] vs. [Ce4+] from 0 to 4 μM (Y = 272.75X − 23.591, R2 = 0.997) and [F − F0] vs. [OCl−] from 0 to 100 μM (Y = 10.915X − 36.818, R2 = 0.997). Where F and F0 are the fluorescence intensity of probes in the presence and absence of analytes [26].

Meanwhile the CIE 1931 [27] chromaticity for FDA and FDH with increasing concentrations of Ce4+ and OCl− were measured. The CIE chromaticity coordinates of FDA displayed from green to pale bluish green with increasing of Ce4+ concentration (Fig. 4b). As shown in Fig. 4(d) for the different concentrations of OCl− corresponding to chromaticity change from pale bluish green to green. This indicates that the interaction between the probes and the ions produce obvious color changes, which can be further applied to the direction of visual inspection.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The Investigation of pH and Fluorescence Decay Curves Measurements
ce:section.ce:para: The influence of pH to fluorescence spectra of probes was investigated in the absence and presence of analytes. In the absence of Ce4+, the fluorescence intensity of FDA displayed continuous increase with the change of pH from 3 to 12. For FDA-Ce4+ system, the fluorescence intensity maintained good stability in the pH range from 3 to 11. When pH was around 8, the optimal fluorescent signal response was observed. FDH itself was not sensitive to pH. The fluorescence rapidly increased and kept stable over the pH range from 4 to 10 when added OCl− to FDH solution (Fig. 5b). The results indicated that the FDA and FDH can be used as probes for Ce4+ and OCl− detection in the weakly acidic, neutral, and basic conditions. Therefore, they have potential applications in biological.

The fluorescence decay curves of FDA and FDH were measured in 10 mM PBS buffer. The decay curves of FDA + Ce4+ and FDH + OCl− are mono-exponential, the life time of FDA + Ce4+ and FDH + OCl− are calculated to be 3.88(τ1) ns (B1 = 815.03) and 3.94(τ1) ns (B1 = 856.93).

The reaction mechanism of FDH and OCl− was further investigated by IR spectra. The lactone (C-COO) absorption of FDH at 1280 and 1752 cm−1 was drastically disappeared which indicated the ring-opened form was formed upon interaction with OCl−. The NH stretching absorption of FDH at 3318 cm−1 was drastically disappeared and a new band positioned at 482 cm−1, which indicated the carbon in binding the Cl formation CCl. Upon the addition of OCl− in FDH, the CN absorption peak at 1589 cm−1 disappeared, which was attributed to the vibration of spiro‑carbon signal (Fig. S3).

Via the Material Studio Suite for standard density functional theory (DFT) [27] calculations FDA and FDH, the absorption and emission characteristics can be further investigated. All data of calculations were obtained by using DMol3 method and simulated in water, the ground and excited state structure of the FDA and FDH were optimized and calculated. Fluorescence properties of FDA and FDH can be elucidated via HOMO, LUMO energy. According to the computing result of FDA, it was observed that the HOMO mainly scattered on the xanthene part, while LUMO is located on the 2,4-dinitroaniline part. After complexes with Ce4+ the HOMO and LUMO of FDA-Ce4+ are distinct with the FDA.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Real Sample Analysis and Sensing Studies With PVDF Membrane
ce:section.ce:para: As shown in Fig. S4, for PVDF membrane based on FDA or FDH, it was observed that the visible color change and different fluorescence. This provides methods of on-site rapid qualitative detection of Ce4+ or OCl−.

Through the MMT experiment, the toxicity of FDA and FDH to VMSCs and its ability to be used in biological applications were evaluated (Fig. S5). The experimental results showed that more than 85% of the cells survived after co-cultivation with 20 μM of FDA or FDH for 24 h, indicating that the probes have little cytotoxicity and can be well applied to biological systems. In order to test the suitability of FDA and FDH in real biological samples, fluorescence imaging experiments were performed in live VMSCs by inverted fluorescence microscope. As shown in Fig. 8 , after co-incubation of 1 μM (FDA) at 37 °C for 2 h, the cells showed green fluorescence (Fig. 8a), which is consistent with the fluorescence of FDA, indicating that FDA has good cell membrane permeability. The fluorescence of VMSCs (pre-incubated with FN-1) were obviously quenched upon the addition 4 μM Ce4+ for 0.5 h (Fig. 8b).

In contrast, after co-incubation of 10 μM (FDH) at 37 °C for 2 h, the cells showed weak fluorescence (Fig. 8c), which is consistent with the fluorescence of FDH. The fluorescence of VMSCs (pre-incubated with FDH) were obviously increased upon the addition100 μM OCl− for 0.5 h (Fig. 8d). The above experimental phenomenon shows that the probes have good biocompatibility and can be applied to detection of Ce4+ and OCl− in living cells.

In summary, two fluorescent probes based on fluorescein-aldehyde and nitroaniline derivatives, FDA and FDH, were synthesized. The difference in the structure of FDA and FDH, which leads to a completely opposite fluorescence phenomenon. FDA can professionally detect Ce4+ in concentration range of 0–4 μM with a limit of 63 nM. FDH contains NN structure, which conforms to the electronic cloud distribution of the PET process. After OCl− was added to the FDH, the fluorescent intensity increased sharply. FDH can efficiently detect OCl− in concentration rang 0–100 μM with a limit of 0.8 μM. The binding mode of FDA with Ce4+ and the sensing mechanism of FDH with OCl− were both verified by density functional theory (DFT). Both FDA and FDH can be successfully used in actual water samples and PVDF membrane. FDA and FDH has water solubility and biocompatibility, so they were successfully applied to biological imaging in VSMCs and are expected to find applications in biosensing.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: The work described in this manuscript was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (51678409, 51638011 and 51508383), Tianjin Research Program of Application Foundation and Advanced Technology (15ZCZDSF00880), State Key Laboratory of Separation Membranes and Membrane Processes (Z1-201507), and the Program for Innovative Research Team in University of Tianjin (TD13-5042).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.saa.2019.01.045.
